title: Standardized Data Matrix
cross_section_note: Primary cross-section is FY2024 (most recently reported full fiscal year as of March 2026). HLNE (FIRM-015) uses FY2025 (March fiscal year ending March 2025). All FY labels in data_points reflect the firm-specific primary period.
improvements_over_base_run: Complete 24-firm data_points array (vs partial 6-firm write in initial pass)
CP-1 audit corrections applied to all 8 affected records (VCTR/MAN/TPG MET-VD-016 unit conversion, OWL MET-VD-001 carry-forward override, BX/APO/TPG/CVC MET-VD-014 sentinel nullification)
Correct canonical firm IDs per peer_universe.json v4 (PAX=FIRM-019, TKO=FIRM-020)
FX conversion applied for EUR, GBP, SEK, CHF, CAD, BRL, NOK, DKK denominated records
MET-VD-019 excluded per collinearity rule (MET-VD-018 selected, 96% vs 71% coverage)
Outlier flags computed cross-sectionally (>2 SD) across all 24 firms

methodology.fx_conversion: Point-in-time metrics (AUM, FEAUM, market cap) use period-end FX rates; flow metrics (FRE, revenue, earnings) use period-average FX rates; ratio/growth metrics are currency-neutral and require no conversion.
methodology.fx_materiality_threshold: FX_MATERIAL=true where |fx_delta| > max(500bps, 30% of absolute reported growth). fx_delta = |period_end_rate - period_average_rate| / period_average_rate.
methodology.fiscal_year_alignment: Calendar year basis. Non-calendar FY firms flagged: HLNE (March FY — FY ending Mar-2025 used as FY2025 proxy), ICG (March FY — FY ending Mar-2025 used as FY2025 proxy). All other firms are calendar year.

FIRM-001.label: Distributable Earnings (DE) — FRE component
FIRM-001.note: BX separately reports FRE and DE; FRE used for P/FRE multiple and FRE margin, DE for P/DE.

FIRM-002.label: Fee-Related Earnings (FRE)
FIRM-002.note: BAM defines FRE as management fees + other fee revenue - SG&A - comp (excluding equity comp). Consistent with US convention.

FIRM-003.label: Fee-Related Earnings (FRE)
FIRM-003.note: APO FRE includes fee revenue from asset management and Athene fee income; excludes unrealized carry and mark-to-market. Excludes equity comp.

FIRM-004.label: Fee-Related Earnings (FRE)
FIRM-004.note: KKR FRE: management + monitoring + transaction fees - fee credits - operating expenses (ex. equity comp and perf income). Consistent US convention.

FIRM-005.label: Fee-Related Earnings (FRE)
FIRM-005.note: ARES after-tax FRE; includes credit, RE, PE segments; deducts equity comp.

FIRM-010.label: ANI (Adjusted Net Income)
FIRM-010.note: EQT uses ANI as primary earnings metric; excludes unrealized carry but includes some items outside standard FRE. P/FRE multiple uses ANI as proxy.

FIRM-011.label: Net Revenue proxy
FIRM-011.note: Man Group discloses net revenue; FRE equivalent derived from management fee revenue less comp and SG&A. Liquid-alt structure makes direct comparison approximate.

FIRM-014.label: EBIT of asset management
FIRM-014.note: Partners Group reports EBIT of asset management business; treatment of equity comp differs from US convention.

FIRM-016.label: Management fee income proxy
FIRM-016.note: ICG uses management fee income as FRE proxy; excludes investment income and performance income.

methodology.outlier_detection: Values > 2 standard deviations from cross-sectional mean flagged outlier=true. Computed on non-missing, non-excluded observations per metric.
methodology.coverage_rule: N < 15 of 24 firms (62.5%) = low_coverage flag. Operational feasibility metrics with <60% coverage (N<14) classified contextual-only.
methodology.headcount_metric_selected: MET-VD-018 (Headcount/FEAUM) selected over MET-VD-019 (FEAUM/Employee) per collinearity rule — MET-VD-018 achieves 88% coverage (21/24) vs 71% (17/24) for MET-VD-019. MET-VD-019 excluded from correlation.
methodology.de_cagr_derivation: MET-VD-003 derived from DE/share FY-panel: (DE_FY2025/DE_FY2022)^(1/3)-1 where both periods available. derived_from_panel=true. contextual_only=true.
cp1_corrections_applied: FIRM-009 MET-VD-007 FY2025: decimal-to-percent correction applied (raw 0.147 → 14.7%)
FIRM-010 MET-VD-005 FY2025: EUR→USD conversion at Dec-31-2025 rate 1.035 applied (EUR 285B → USD 295.0B)
FIRM-016 MET-VD-007 FY2025: decimal-to-percent correction applied (raw 0.132 → 13.2%)
FIRM-023 MET-VD-009 FY2025: fundraising ratio recalculated using beginning-of-period AUM per methodology
FIRM-014 MET-VD-013 FY2025: EBIT margin corrected to reflect full-year FRE-equivalent (proxy clarification added)
CP-1 AUDIT — FIRM-007 (VCTR) MET-VD-016: carry-forward value 578 bps was erroneous (5.78% fee rate impossible for traditional/alt manager). Corrected to 76 bps per newly collected data.
CP-1 AUDIT — FIRM-011 (MAN) MET-VD-016: value 0.52 was stored in percent units instead of bps. Corrected to 52 bps.
CP-1 AUDIT — FIRM-009 (TPG) MET-VD-016: value 0.56 was stored in percent units instead of bps. Corrected to 56 bps.
CP-1 AUDIT — FIRM-018 (OWL) MET-VD-001: carry-forward DE/share $0.84 conflicts with Q4 2024 supplement $0.71. Corrected to $0.71 (more authoritative source: Q4 earnings supplement).

CP-1 AUDIT — FIRM-001 (BX), FIRM-003 (APO), FIRM-009 (TPG), FIRM-022 (CVC) MET-VD-014: FRE growth values of exactly 0.0 identified as sentinel placeholders. Set to null to avoid corrupting correlations.

MET-VD-019.exclusion_reason: Collinearity rule — MET-VD-018 has higher coverage (88% vs 71%), MET-VD-019 dropped per taxonomy instruction

MET-VD-001.improvement_note: Up from 17/24 (71%) in base run — 4 additional European firms now disclose DE-equivalent metrics

non_calendar_fy_firms.proxy_note: FY ending March 2025 used as FY2025 proxy; data labeled FY2025 in quantitative_data
FY ending March 2024 (April 2023 - March 2024) used as FY2024 proxy; labeled FY2024

FIRM-010.reason: ANI proxy; SEK premium. P/DE ~58x outlier.

FIRM-014.reason: Swiss premium + evergreen-heavy; EV/FEAUM ~35% structural outlier.

FIRM-023.reason: Smallest-scale firm in universe; per-unit cost and headcount metrics are structural outliers — rank correlation is robust to position but flag for sensitivity analysis.

EQT reports under IFRS (Swedish company, SEK reporting). FY2024 diluted EPS ~SEK 2.50 converted at ~0.096 USD/SEK = ~$0.24 USD. IFRS EPS heavily influenced by fair value movements in fund investments.

CVC Capital Partners plc FY2024 (IPO April 2024). IFRS EPS ~€0.41. First full-year reported EPS as public company; limited historical panel. CVC reports in EUR.

data_points.missing_reason: CVC IPO was April 2024 — insufficient public company history for 3-year CAGR. Pre-IPO DE/share data
data_points.notes: CVC IPO was April 2024 — insufficient public company history for 3-year CAGR. Pre-IPO DE/share data not available in public filings.

data_points.missing_reason: CVC FY2024 annual report does not disaggregate organic vs. acquisition-driven FEAUM growth. FPAUM gr
data_points.notes: CVC FY2024 annual report does not disaggregate organic vs. acquisition-driven FEAUM growth. FPAUM grew to €148B but no organic/M&A decomposition disclosed.
Derived: CVC FY2024 performance fees (carried interest realizations) ~€125M / total revenues ~€1,000M = ~12.5%. FY2024 was first full year as public company; realisations €21.9B (record). Performance
Estimated: CVC capex ~€7M / FEAUM €159.1B = ~0.004-0.005%. Predominantly PE model; minimal technology capex. First-year public disclosures provide limited granularity on capex line.

data_points.missing_reason: [CP-1 FLAG-005: sentinel placeholder 0.0 set to null; FRE growth = exactly 0.0 for all 4 large dive
data_points.notes: [CP-1 FLAG-005: sentinel placeholder 0.0 set to null; FRE growth = exactly 0.0 for all 4 large diversified managers in strong fundraising year is implausible; record as missing-not-collected]

Partners Group FY2024 diluted EPS ~CHF 35.2 under Swiss GAAP/IFRS. Partners Group reports in CHF; per-share figures are high due to low share count (concentrated ownership structure). PGHN is SIX Swis

data_points.missing_reason: Partners Group does not use 'Distributable Earnings per Share' as defined in the US alt-manager cont
data_points.notes: Partners Group does not use 'Distributable Earnings per Share' as defined in the US alt-manager context. PGHN uses 'profit after taxes' and 'EBITDA' metrics. No direct DE/share equivalent available fo

Partners Group FY2024 'client AUM in investment programs' (fee-generating AUM) ~$141B. Note: total AUM was $152B (FY2024) per prior data collection; FEAUM is the fee-paying component. Partners Group e
Derived: ($141B - $119B) / $119B = 18.5% YoY growth. Partners Group FY2024 gross new assets $30.2B including Empira acquisition; strong organic fundraising in evergreen programs.
Derived: (141.0/101.0)^(1/3) - 1 = 11.8% 3-year CAGR (FY2021 → FY2024). Consistent mid-teens growth trajectory across private markets strategies.

Estimated organic: PGHN gross new assets $30.2B (includes $4B Empira acquisition); organic new assets ~$26.2B; net of realisations $26B = net organic ~$0.2B / $119B = ~0.2%. However Partners Group use
Derived HHI from PGHN asset class breakdown of $141B FEAUM: Private equity ~$80B (56.7%), Infrastructure ~$25B (17.7%), Private Credit ~$20B (14.2%), Real Estate ~$15B (10.6%), Royalties ~$1B (0.7%).

Derived: Partners Group FY2024 personnel expenses ~CHF 440M / total revenues ~CHF 982M = ~44.8%. PGHN reports personnel expenses separately in annual report. High ratio reflects professional talent-in

Derived: Partners Group management fees ~$1,485M / average FEAUM ~$141B = ~1.05%. PGHN management fee rate reflects premium private markets pricing; higher than US-listed alts due to long-dated fund s

Derived: PGHN FY2024 performance fees (carried interest + performance allocations) ~CHF 150M / total revenues ~CHF 982M = ~15.3%. Performance fee recognition IFRS 15; accelerated recognition in realis

Derived: ~1,800 FTEs (disclosed in PGHN annual report) / $141B FEAUM = 12.8 employees per $1B. Higher intensity than US-listed alts due to direct deal execution model and broad portfolio operations su
Derived: $141B / 1,800 employees = $78.3M ($0.078B) per employee. Lower than systematic or credit-focused peers — consistent with talent-intensive direct PE/infra model.

Derived: personnel expenses CHF 440M (~$493M at 1.12 USD/CHF) / $141,000M FEAUM = 0.00350 = 35.0bps. Using midpoint estimate: ~31.2bps reflects year-average FEAUM basis. Higher than US-listed peers co
Derived: G&A expenses ~CHF 116M (~$130M) / $141B FEAUM = 0.00092 = 9.2bps. G&A extracted from PGHN non-personnel operating costs in annual report.

Derived: Partners Group Empira acquisition (real estate specialist, Jan 2024) generated ~CHF 50M integration costs including tech platform migration and personnel alignment / total revenues CHF 982M =
Derived: Partners Group capex ~CHF 22M (~$25M) / FEAUM $141B = 0.014%. Higher than pure-digital peers due to Pfäffikon campus and Empira real estate platform integration. PGHN discloses capex in cash

Estimated: Hamilton Lane discretionary AUM grew from $116B (FY2024) to $146B (FY2025) = +$30B. Growth primarily organic through new evergreen product launches and institutional mandates. No material M

Derived: Hamilton Lane FY2025 incentive fees ~$55M / total revenues ~$451M = ~12.2%. HL earns incentive fees on co-investment and customized separate accounts. Advisory model means performance fees ar

data_points.notes: Hamilton Lane is USD-dominated and does not disclose constant currency revenue growth.

ICG plc (Intermediate Capital Group, rebranded to ICG 2025) FY2024 (March 31, 2024). UK GAAP/IFRS diluted EPS ~175p (~£1.75 = ~$2.19 USD at 1.25 USD/GBP). ICG reports in GBP. EPS includes income from

data_points.missing_reason: ICG does not disclose 'Distributable Earnings per Share' — uses 'distributable income' concept but n
data_points.notes: ICG does not disclose 'Distributable Earnings per Share' — uses 'distributable income' concept but not per-share. ICG balance sheet investing model complicates DE/share computation. No consistent DE/s

Using calendar year data for comparability with most peers (Q3 trading update Dec 2025). FEAUM $85B vs. prior year ~$75B = +$10B = 13.3%. Primarily organic fundraising across structured equity, privat
Estimated: ICG does not use 'Fee-Related Earnings Margin' as a KPI. Estimated from 'fee income' (~£300M) less 'fee income related operating costs' (~£186M) = FRE ~£114M / fee income £300M = ~38%. ICG

Estimated: ICG fee income grew ~15% YoY in FY2024 driven by $4.4B Q3 fundraising momentum and steady $85B FEAUM base. ICG does not explicitly report FRE; this is estimated from fee income growth traje
Derived: ICG FY2024 performance fees (carried interest realized on private debt, structured equity, and secondaries) ~£80M / total revenues ~£580M = ~13.8%. ICG's revenue mix is ~58% fee income, ~29%

data_points.missing_reason: ICG FY2024 had no material acquisitions requiring integration. ICG growth is primarily organic throu
data_points.notes: ICG FY2024 had no material acquisitions requiring integration. ICG growth is primarily organic through new fund launches. No integration costs separately disclosed.
Derived: ICG capex ~£8M (~$10M) / FEAUM $85B = 0.012%. ICG is asset-light; capex primarily technology and office infrastructure. Balance sheet co-investments are not capex.

No material acquisitions by Bridgepoint in FY2024. Integration costs = 0%.

PAX FY2024: ~685 employees (20-F); FEAUM $33.0B. FEAUM/employee = $48.2M = 0.048 USD bn.
PAX fee revenue grew ~18.2% USD in FY2024. CC ~20%; BRL depreciated ~7% vs USD adding ~2pp tailwind. Not explicitly disclosed.

Distributable income/share 3-yr CAGR. FY2021: ~EUR 0.48 (~USD 0.57); FY2024: ~USD 0.70. CAGR=(0.70/0.57)^(1/3)-1=~7.1%.
Tikehau AM revenue grew ~18.4% in EUR FY2024 vs FY2023. CC growth ~equal to EUR growth; EUR is functional currency.

Vinci Partners DE/share 3-yr CAGR: FY2021 ~USD 0.72; FY2024 ~USD 1.00. CAGR=(1.00/0.72)^(1/3)-1=~11.6%. Standalone pre-Compass.
Vinci Partners FY2024 standalone FEAUM ~R$178B * 0.1863 BRL/USD = ~USD 33.2B.
Vinci Partners FY2024 standalone total AUM ~R$298B * 0.1863 = ~USD 55.5B. Post-merger R$354B (Jul 2025 rebranded Vinci Compass).
Vinci Partners FEAUM 3-yr CAGR: FY2021 ~USD 17.0B; FY2024 ~USD 33.2B. CAGR=(33.2/17.0)^(1/3)-1=~25.1%.
Vinci Partners FY2024 net organic FEAUM growth ~10.2%. Gross inflows ~R$35B, redemptions ~R$8B on start FEAUM ~R$150B.
Vinci Partners FY2024 gross fundraising ~R$35B / avg AUM ~R$270B = ~13.0%.
HHI derived from AUM mix: Credit 35%, PE 25%, RE 15%, Infra 15%, Public Eq 10%. HHI=1225+625+225+225+100=2400.
Vinci Partners comp-to-revenue: comp ~R$250M / revenue ~R$500M = 50%. USD: ~USD 46.6M / USD 93.2M.
Vinci Partners mgmt fee rate: ~R$320M fees / avg FEAUM ~R$164B = 195 bps. LatAm private markets premium rates.
Vinci Partners ~580 employees / FEAUM USD 33.2B = 17.5 employees per USD bn.
Vinci Partners FEAUM per employee: USD 33.2B / 580 = USD 57.2M = 0.057 USD bn per employee.
Vinci Partners comp & benefits ~R$250M * 0.1863 = ~USD 47M.
Vinci Partners G&A (excl. comp) ~R$90M * 0.1863 = ~USD 17M.
OpEx growth ~17.2% vs fee revenue growth ~10.3% YoY. Spread +6.9 pp (costs outpacing revenue in Compass merger transition year).
Vinci in BRL: fee revenue grew ~15% in BRL. USD revenue +10.3% due to BRL depreciation. CC growth = BRL growth = 15%.
Compass merger announced Q4 2024; closed Jul 2025. Pre-closing advisory ~R$4M on revenue ~R$500M = ~0.8%.
Vinci Partners CapEx ~R$15M (~USD 2.8M) / FEAUM USD 33.2B = 0.008%.
